primary_variable.file: Hughes et al. 2019 - Neural adaptation to faces reveals racial outgroup homogeneity effects in early perception.pdf
primary_variable.title: Neural adaptation to faces reveals racial outgroup homogeneity effects in early perception
primary_variable.evidence: We investigated the neural mechanisms of this effect by testing whether high-level visual cortex is differentially tuned in its sensitivity to variation in own-race versus other-race faces.

primary_variable.file: Contreras et al. 2013 - Multivoxel patterns in fusiform face area differentiate faces by sex and race.pdf
primary_variable.title: Multivoxel Patterns in Fusiform Face Area Differentiate Faces by Sex and Race
primary_variable.evidence: Multivoxel patterns in FFA differentiated faces by sex and race. Patterns were more similar between individuals of the same race than between Black and White individuals.

primary_variable.file: Golby et al. 2001 - Differential responses in the fusiform region to same-race and other-race faces.pdf
primary_variable.title: Differential responses in the fusiform region to same-race and other-race faces
primary_variable.evidence: We investigated the neural substrates underlying the differences in memory for same-race and other-race faces using fMRI in African-American and European-American participants.

primary_variable.file: Martin et al. 2025 - Racial Differences in Small Vessel Disease Measured in MRI of Ischemic Stroke - TIA Patients.pdf
primary_variable.title: Racial Differences in Small Vessel Disease Measured in MRI of Ischemic Stroke/TIA Patients
primary_variable.evidence: AA race was independently associated with moderate/severe PVWMH (OR 2.04), DWMH (OR 1.69), and CMB≥5 (OR 4.44) after adjusting for vascular risk factors.

primary_variable.file: Rubien-Thomas et al. 2021 - Processing of Task-Irrelevant Race Informati ... ated with Diminished Cognitive Control in Black and White Individuals.pdf
primary_variable.title: Processing of Task-Irrelevant Race Information is Associated with Diminished Cognitive Control in Black and White Individuals
primary_variable.evidence: Examined how task-irrelevant race information influences cognitive control in Black (n=54) and White (n=51) participants, comparing neural and behavioral effects across racial groups.

primary_variable.file: Reggev et al. 2020 - Human Face-Selective Cortex Does Not Distinguish between Members of a Racial Outgroup.pdf
primary_variable.title: Human Face-Selective Cortex Does Not Distinguish between Members of a Racial Outgroup
primary_variable.evidence: White perceivers' fusiform face area distinguished different faces only for racial ingroup (White) but not outgroup (Black) members, demonstrating neural basis of outgroup homogeneity effect.

primary_variable.file: Krosch and Amodio 2019 - Scarcity disrupts the neural encoding of Black faces - A socioperceptual pathway to discrimination.pdf
primary_variable.title: Scarcity Disrupts the Neural Encoding of Black Faces: A Socioperceptual Pathway to Discrimination
primary_variable.evidence: Scarcity selectively impairs neural encoding of Black faces relative to White faces, indexed by delayed N170 and reduced fusiform activity, predicting anti-Black allocation decisions.

primary_variable.file: Caldito et al. 2018 - Brain and retinal atrophy in African-Americans versus Caucasian-Americans with multiple sclerosis - a longitudinal study.pdf
primary_variable.title: Brain and retinal atrophy in African-Americans versus Caucasian-Americans with multiple sclerosis: a longitudinal study
primary_variable.evidence: African Americans with multiple sclerosis demonstrate higher inflammatory disease activity, faster disability accumulation, and more pronounced brain tissue damage compared to Caucasian Americans.

disparities_causal.file: Elbasheir et al. 2024 - Racial Discrimination, Neural Connectivity, and Epigenetic Aging Among Black Women.pdf
disparities_causal.title: Racial Discrimination, Neural Connectivity, and Epigenetic Aging Among Black Women
disparities_causal.evidence: Racial discrimination increases risk of adverse brain health outcomes, potentially via neuroplastic changes; examined associations of racial discrimination with brainstem and midbrain resting-state functional connectivity and DNA methylatio

disparities_causal.file: Chen et al. 2025 - Perceived Racism, Brain Development, and Internalizing and Externalizing Symptoms - Findings From the ABCD Study.pdf
disparities_causal.title: Perceived Racism, Brain Development, and Internalizing and Externalizing Symptoms: Findings From the ABCD Study
disparities_causal.evidence: Racial discrimination drives health disparities...creating chronic stress that affects brain development...examined neurobiological mechanisms linking discrimination to mental and behavioral health outcomes.

disparities_causal.file: Acosta-Rodriguez et al. 2025 - Impact of Childhood Neighborhood Deprivation on White Matter and Functional Connectivity During Adolescence.pdf
disparities_causal.title: Impact of Childhood Neighborhood Deprivation on White Matter and Functional Connectivity During Adolescence
disparities_causal.evidence: neighborhood deprivation (ADI), often measured by the Area Deprivation Index, may influence neurodevelopment...children living in poorer neighborhoods had decreased connectivity

disparities_causal.file: Bayoumi et al. 2025 - Ethnoracial disparities in gray matter atrophy are mediated by structural disconnectivity in multiple sclerosis.pdf
disparities_causal.title: Ethnoracial disparities in gray matter atrophy are mediated by structural disconnectivity in multiple sclerosis
disparities_causal.evidence: Mediation analyses revealed that lesion load significantly mediated group differences in global GM atrophy and structural disconnectivity mediated some differences in specific gray matter components.

disparities_causal.file: Benson et al. 2025 - What's the harm - Examining police contact and amygdala reactivity among black adolescents.pdf
disparities_causal.title: What's the harm? Examining police contact and amygdala reactivity among black adolescents
disparities_causal.evidence: Police contact conceptualized as racialized adversity embedded in institutional racism; models police contact as a causal exposure predicting amygdala reactivity in Black adolescents.

disparities_causal.file: Beydoun et al. 2024 - Uncovering mediational pathways behind racial and ... mic disparities in brain volumes - insights from the UK Biobank study.pdf
disparities_causal.title: Uncovering mediational pathways behind racial and socioeconomic disparities in brain volumes: insights from the UK Biobank study
disparities_causal.evidence: We examined racial/ethnic and SES disparities in sMRI markers and tested total, direct, and indirect effects through lifestyle, health-related, and cognition factors using structural equations modeling.

disparities_causal.file: Bhogal et al. 2025 - Racial Discrimination and Fear-Based Symptoms - Links to Avoidance Behavior and Hippocampal Activity in Black Youth.pdf
disparities_causal.title: Racial Discrimination and Fear-Based Symptoms: Links to Avoidance Behavior and Hippocampal Activity in Black Youth
disparities_causal.evidence: RD modeled as psychosocial stressor affecting fear extinction and brain activity; mechanisms explored through hippocampal mediation of RD-avoidance association.

disparities_causal.file: Palms et al. 2025 - The relationship between racial discrimination and white matter among Black older adults.pdf
disparities_causal.title: The relationship between racial discrimination and white matter among Black older adults
disparities_causal.evidence: Racial discrimination activates physiological stress responses leading to downstream health effects. White matter may be a mechanism through which racially patterned stressors contribute to disparities in brain and cognitive aging.

disparities_descriptive.file: Gottesman et al. 2015 - Brain Aging in African-Americans - The Atherosclerosis Risk in Communities (ARIC) Experience.pdf
disparities_descriptive.title: Brain aging in African-Americans: The Atherosclerosis Risk in Communities (ARIC) experience
disparities_descriptive.evidence: Disparities in stroke rates and stroke-related mortality are commonly reported with higher rates among African-American individuals than among whites. African-Americans appear at increased risk for adverse subclinical brain changes and deme

disparities_descriptive.file: Hayes et al. 2025 - Racial ethnic variations in the cardiometabolic dete ... sure of white matter hyperintensities among females-The HABS-HD Study.pdf
disparities_descriptive.title: Racial ethnic variations in the cardiometabolic determinants and blood pressure of white matter hyperintensities among females—The HABS-HD Study
disparities_descriptive.evidence: Study examines racial/ethnic differences in cardiometabolic contributions to WMH burden, highlighting disparities and emphasizing need for tailored prevention strategies in diverse female populations.

disparities_descriptive.file: Kellier et al. 2025 - Association of Race and Ethnicity With Emergency R ... Migraine Diagnosis, Testing, and Management in Children With Headache.pdf
disparities_descriptive.title: Association of Race and Ethnicity With Emergency Room Rate of Migraine Diagnosis, Testing, and Management in Children With Headache
disparities_descriptive.evidence: NHB and HL children receive fewer migraine diagnoses, less testing, and less intensive treatment compared with NHW children in pediatric ED.

disparities_descriptive.file: Williams et al. 2023 - Demographics and baseline disease characteristics ... sis in the open-label, single-arm, multicenter, phase IV CHIMES trial.pdf
disparities_descriptive.title: Demographics and baseline disease characteristics of Black and Hispanic patients with multiple sclerosis in the open-label, single-arm, multicenter, phase IV CHIMES trial
disparities_descriptive.evidence: BpwMS and HpwMS exhibit greater disease severity and more rapid disease progression than WpwMS. Data from CHIMES revealed differences in demographics and baseline disease characteristics between BpwMS and HpwMS vs WpwMS.

disparities_descriptive.file: O'Bryant et al. 2021 - A blood screening tool for detecting mild cogniti ... d non-Hispanic Whites - A method for increasing representation o[...].pdf
disparities_descriptive.title: A blood screening tool for detecting mild cognitive impairment and Alzheimer's disease among community-dwelling Mexican Americans and non-Hispanic Whites: A method for increasing representation of diverse populations in clinical research
disparities_descriptive.evidence: Representation of Mexican Americans in Alzheimer's disease clinical research has been extremely poor...novel tools are needed to increase representation of Mexican Americans in AD observational studies and clinical trials.

disparities_descriptive.file: Prabhakaran et al. 2007 - Prevalence and determinants of subclinical brain infarction - the Northern Manhattan Study.pdf
disparities_descriptive.title: The prevalence and determinants of subclinical brain infarction: the Northern Manhattan Study
disparities_descriptive.evidence: SBI prevalence greater among blacks (24.0%) vs whites (18.1%) and Hispanics (15.8%); blacks under 65 had disproportionately higher SBI proportions.

disparities_descriptive.file: McGruder et al. 2006 - Characteristics of an 'ill-defined' diagnosis for stroke - opportunities for improvement.pdf
disparities_descriptive.title: Characteristics of an "Ill-Defined" Diagnosis for Stroke: Opportunities for Improvement
disparities_descriptive.evidence: After controlling for age, blacks (OR, 1.31) and Hispanics (OR, 1.27) were more likely to receive ill-defined stroke diagnosis compared with whites.

disparities_descriptive.file: Goldstein et al. 2003 - Veterans Administration Acute Stroke (VASt) Stud ... d differences in utilization of stroke-related procedures or services.pdf
disparities_descriptive.title: Veterans Administration Acute Stroke (VASt) Study: Lack of Race/Ethnic-Based Differences in Utilization of Stroke-Related Procedures or Services
disparities_descriptive.evidence: Race/ethnic-based disparities in stroke care utilization documented in VA system with equal-access healthcare, finding minimal differences except ECG and angiography.

methodological_critique.file: Dewey et al. 2019 - DeepHarmony - A deep learning approach to contrast harmonization across scanner changes.pdf
methodological_critique.title: DeepHarmony: A deep learning approach to contrast harmonization across scanner changes
methodological_critique.evidence: The paper does not discuss or analyze race/ethnicity. It focuses on technical MRI harmonization methods across scanner protocols in MS patients and healthy subjects.

methodological_critique.file: Gaj et al. 2025 - Subject-Based Transfer Learning in Longitudinal Multiple Sclerosis Lesion Segmentation.pdf
methodological_critique.title: Subject-Based Transfer Learning in Longitudinal Multiple Sclerosis Lesion Segmentation
methodological_critique.evidence: No race or ethnicity mentioned in title, abstract, or introduction. Paper focuses on transfer learning methodology for MS lesion segmentation.

methodological_critique.file: Orchard et al. 2025 - Scanning for Representation - A Scoping Review of Racial and Ethnic Diversity in MRI Studies of the Maternal Brain.pdf
methodological_critique.title: Scanning for Representation: A Scoping Review of Racial and Ethnic Diversity in MRI Studies of the Maternal Brain
methodological_critique.evidence: Scoping review examining racial and ethnic diversity reporting in MRI studies of the maternal brain, critiquing underreporting (38.38%), overrepresentation of white participants (46.83%), and geographic bias in neuroimaging research practic

methodological_critique.file: Oyeniran et al. 2025 - Representation in Brain Imaging Research - A Quebec Demographic Overview.pdf
methodological_critique.title: Representation in Brain Imaging Research: A Quebec Demographic Overview
methodological_critique.evidence: Race and ethnicity were most poorly documented, with fewer than 4% of studies reporting this information. Among 2,396 participants with recorded race/ethnicity, 94.2% were White, highlighting mismatch with Quebec's population diversity.

methodological_critique.file: Bissett et al. 2021 - Design issues and solutions for stop-signal data from the Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development (ABCD) study.pdf
methodological_critique.title: Design issues and solutions for stop-signal data from the Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development (ABCD) study
methodological_critique.evidence: Design issues in ABCD stop-signal task significantly compromise utility for understanding response inhibition; proposes solutions for experimental code and data analysis.

methodological_critique.file: Bolla et al. 2026 - Brain volumetric variability and artificial intellig ... ference standards and social determinant adjustment. A scoping review.pdf
methodological_critique.title: Brain volumetric variability and artificial intelligence diagnosis: Importance of race/ethnicity-specific reference standards and social determinant adjustment. A scoping review
methodological_critique.evidence: Critically examines how racial, ethnic, and social determinants influence brain volumetric variability; emphasizes bias mitigation, population-sensitive standards, and ethical risks in AI-based neuroimaging.

methodological_critique.file: Lal Khakpoor et al. 2025 - When Brain Models Aren't Universal - Benchmarking of Ethnic Bias in MRI-Based Cognitive Prediction Across Modalities.pdf
methodological_critique.title: Brain predictive models of cognition fail to generalize across ethnicities: Modality-dependent bias in MRI-based prediction
methodological_critique.evidence: This first modality-wide benchmark reveals pervasive, modality-dependent ethnic bias in cognitive prediction and identifies key factors shaping equity in neuroimaging models.

methodological_critique.file: Fields et al. 2025 - Governance for anti-racist AI in healthcare - integrating racism-related stress in psychiatric algorithms for Black Americans.pdf
methodological_critique.title: Governance for anti-racist AI in healthcare: integrating racism-related stress in psychiatric algorithms for Black Americans
methodological_critique.evidence: Proposes framework for anti-racist AI governance in psychiatry; critiques algorithmic bias and failure to account for racism-related stress in clinical algorithms for Black Americans.

nuisance_covariate.file: Kohno et al. 2026 - Association of MRI-Visible Perivascular Spaces With Longitudinal Cognitive Decline Over a Decade.pdf
nuisance_covariate.title: Association of MRI-Visible Perivascular Spaces With Longitudinal Cognitive Decline Over a Decade
nuisance_covariate.evidence: race/ethnicity entered as adjustment variable in linear mixed-effects model; no race effect interpreted or discussed

nuisance_covariate.file: Na et al. 2026 - Maternal physical activity during pregnancy is associat ... in cortical development and executive function in 8-year-old children.pdf
nuisance_covariate.title: Maternal physical activity during pregnancy is associated with changes of brain cortical development and executive function in 8-year-old children
nuisance_covariate.evidence: race, BMI, parental socioeconomic status and childhood traumatic experience controlled.

nuisance_covariate.file: Canning et al. 2025 - Associations between life course exposure to ambie ... structure - a population-based study of the 1946 British Birth Cohort.pdf
nuisance_covariate.title: Associations between life course exposure to ambient air pollution with cognition and later-life brain structure: a population-based study of the 1946 British Birth Cohort
nuisance_covariate.evidence: Adjusted for sociodemographic factors including smoking status and neighbourhood deprivation. Race not mentioned in objectives, results, or interpretation.

nuisance_covariate.file: Chen et al. 2025 - The effects of polygenic risks for alcohol misuse on negative emotion processing in young adult binge drinkers.pdf
nuisance_covariate.title: The effects of polygenic risks for alcohol misuse on negative emotion processing in young adult binge drinkers
nuisance_covariate.evidence: age, sex, and race as covariates...controlling for age, sex, and race in whole-brain regressions and ROI analyses.

nuisance_covariate.file: Chen et al. 2025 - Polygenic risk for depression and resting-state functional connectivity of subgenual anterior cingulate cortex in young adults.pdf
nuisance_covariate.title: Polygenic risk for depression and resting-state functional connectivity of subgenual anterior cingulate cortex in young adults
nuisance_covariate.evidence: Controlling for age, sex, race or ethnicity, alcohol use severity, and household income in whole-brain regressions; race entered as adjustment variable with no interpretation of race effects.

nuisance_covariate.file: Dintica et al. 2024 - Life's Essential 8 and midlife trajectories in cognition and brain health - The CARDIA study.pdf
nuisance_covariate.title: Life's Essential 8 and midlife trajectories in cognition and brain health: The CARDIA study
nuisance_covariate.evidence: Models adjusted for demographic factors (age, sex, race, and education). Race is entered as adjustment variable in mixed effects regression, not interpreted as effect of interest.

nuisance_covariate.file: Eagle et al. 2025 - Identifying a Biological Signature of Trauma-Related ... wing Repeated Traumatic Brain Injuries Compared with Healthy Controls.pdf
nuisance_covariate.title: Identifying a Biological Signature of Trauma-Related Neurodegeneration Following Repeated Traumatic Brain Injuries Compared with Healthy Controls
nuisance_covariate.evidence: Age, years of education, military status, biological sex, race/ethnicity, and total self-reported TBIs were included as covariates in all models with Bonferroni corrections.

nuisance_covariate.file: Hidalgo-Lopez et al. 2025 - Sex, Neural Networks, and Behavioral Symptoms Among Adolescents With Multisite Pain.pdf
nuisance_covariate.title: Sex, Neural Networks, and Behavioral Symptoms Among Adolescents With Multisite Pain
nuisance_covariate.evidence: Participants were matched by pubertal status, handedness, and race and ethnicity. Race appears in demographics table and matching procedure but no race effects are interpreted.

matching_variable.file: Betz et al. 2026 - Sleep Disturbances and Cognition, Behavior, and Brain Structure in Children With mTBI.pdf
matching_variable.title: Sleep Disturbances and Cognition, Behavior, and Brain Structure in Children With mTBI
matching_variable.evidence: Children with mTBI were propensity score–matched on the basis of age, sex, study site, self-reported race, and total family income to typically developing children and orthopedic injury controls.

matching_variable.file: Huang et al. 2022 - World Trade Center Site Exposure Duration Is Associated with Hippocampal and Cerebral White Matter Neuroinflammation.pdf
matching_variable.title: World Trade Center Site Exposure Duration Is Associated with Hippocampal and Cerebral White Matter Neuroinflammation
matching_variable.evidence: Responder participants were matched on age, sex, race/ethnicity, occupation, and education at the time of screening.

matching_variable.file: Li et al. 2023 - Morphological abnormalities in youth with bipolar disorder and their relationship to clinical characteristics.pdf
matching_variable.title: Morphological Abnormalities in Youth with Bipolar Disorder and their Relationship to Clinical Characteristics
matching_variable.evidence: Healthy comparison adolescents were matched for age, race, sex, socioeconomic status, intelligence quotient (IQ), and education level.

matching_variable.file: Sen et al. 2023 - Cerebral blood flow in patients recovered from mild COVID-19.pdf
matching_variable.title: Cerebral blood flow in patients recovered from mild COVID-19
matching_variable.evidence: age-, sex-, and race-matched healthy controls were drawn from the Aging Brain Consortium. Race appears in demographic matching criteria but is not analyzed as a study variable.

matching_variable.file: Zakiniaeiz et al. 2023 - Diagnostic group differences and exploratory se ... ring fMRI Stroop in individuals with and without cocaine use disorder.pdf
matching_variable.title: Diagnostic group differences and exploratory sex differences in intrinsic connectivity during fMRI Stroop in individuals with and without cocaine use disorder
matching_variable.evidence: 40 individuals with CUD (20 female) and 40 HC (20 female) subjects matched on age, race, and ethnicity completed an fMRI Stroop task.

matching_variable.file: Redel et al. 2022 - Cerebral blood flow is lower in youth with type 2 diabetes compared to obese controls - A pilot study.pdf
matching_variable.title: Cerebral blood flow is lower in youth with type 2 diabetes compared to obese controls: A pilot study
matching_variable.evidence: Control group was selected to be similar in age, sex, race, and BMI to the type 2 diabetes group.

matching_variable.file: Anteraper et al. 2021 - A comprehensive data-driven analysis framework f ... networks with resting state fMRI in HIV-infected individuals on cART.pdf
matching_variable.title: A comprehensive data-driven analysis framework for detecting impairments in brain function networks with resting state fMRI in HIV-infected individuals on cART
matching_variable.evidence: 16 HIV-positive subjects and 12 age, sex, and race matched HIV seronegative controls. African-American: 15 (94%) vs 10 (83%), p=0.56.

matching_variable.file: Cobia et al. 2021 - Basal ganglia shape features differentiate schizoaffective disorder from schizophrenia.pdf
matching_variable.title: Basal Ganglia Shape Features Differentiate Schizoaffective Disorder from Schizophrenia
matching_variable.evidence: A subset of SCZ and CON subjects were selected from a larger sample to specifically match against SA subjects with respect to age, gender, parental socioeconomic status, and race.

sample_descriptor_only.file: Rosen et al. 2002 - Variable effects of aging on frontal lobe contributions to memory.pdf
sample_descriptor_only.title: Variable effects of aging on frontal lobe contributions to memory
sample_descriptor_only.evidence: No mention of race or ethnicity in title, abstract, introduction, methods, or results. Not used analytically or descriptively.

sample_descriptor_only.file: Herm et al. 2019 - MRI Brain Changes After Marathon Running - Results of the Berlin Beat of Running Study.pdf
sample_descriptor_only.title: MRI Brain Changes After Marathon Running: Results of the Berlin Beat of Running Study
sample_descriptor_only.evidence: No mention of race or ethnicity in abstract, introduction, methods, or results. Race does not appear as a variable, covariate, or matching criterion.

sample_descriptor_only.file: Nistri et al. 2026 - Investigating the Frequency and Outcome of Central Vein Sign and Paramagnetic Rim Lesions in Children With MOGAD.pdf
sample_descriptor_only.title: Investigating the Frequency and Outcome of Central Vein Sign and Paramagnetic Rim Lesions in Children With MOGAD
sample_descriptor_only.evidence: No mention of race or ethnicity in title, abstract, introduction, or methods. Demographics table reports age, sex, clinical features only.

sample_descriptor_only.file: Toljan et al. 2026 - Diagnostic performance of central vein sign and paramagnetic rim lesion integration for multiple sclerosis.pdf
sample_descriptor_only.title: Diagnostic performance of central vein sign and paramagnetic rim lesion integration for multiple sclerosis
sample_descriptor_only.evidence: Seventy-eight participants were included (71% female, 86% white, 37 with MS).

sample_descriptor_only.file: Aiello et al. 2025 - Microstructural assessment of the locus coeruleus-e ... rtex pathway and association with ATN markers in cognitive impairment.pdf
sample_descriptor_only.title: Microstructural assessment of the locus coeruleus–entorhinal cortex pathway and association with ATN markers in cognitive impairment
sample_descriptor_only.evidence: No mention of race or ethnicity in title, abstract, introduction, methods, or demographics table (Table 1).

sample_descriptor_only.file: Amin et al. 2024 - Incorporation of the central vein sign into the McDonald criteria.pdf
sample_descriptor_only.title: Incorporation of the central vein sign into the McDonald criteria
sample_descriptor_only.evidence: Table 1 lists baseline demographics including age, sex, disease duration, and comorbidities; race/ethnicity absent from demographics table and paper text.

sample_descriptor_only.file: Bernstein et al. 2025 - Periventricular gradients in axonal and myelin m ... sent in people with multiple sclerosis having low and high disability.pdf
sample_descriptor_only.title: Periventricular gradients in axonal and myelin microstructure are present in people with multiple sclerosis having low and high disability
sample_descriptor_only.evidence: Demographics summarized in Table 1; no race/ethnicity data reported or analyzed.

sample_descriptor_only.file: Detcheverry et al. 2025 - Investigating the oxidative stress-vascular brain injury axis in mild cognitive impairment of the Alzheimer's type.pdf
sample_descriptor_only.title: Investigating the oxidative stress–vascular brain injury axis in mild cognitive impairment of the Alzheimer's type
sample_descriptor_only.evidence: 31 MCI participants (20 female) recruited from Jewish General Hospital Memory Clinic. No mention of race or ethnicity in demographics, methods, or analysis.

false_positive.file: Zanchi et al. 2017 - Extreme Mountain Ultra-Marathon Leads to Acute but ... se in Cerebral Water Diffusivity and Plasma Biomarkers Levels Changes.pdf
false_positive.title: Extreme Mountain Ultra-Marathon Leads to Acute but Transient Increase in Cerebral Water Diffusivity and Plasma Biomarkers Levels Changes
false_positive.evidence: No race or ethnicity mentioned in title, abstract, or methods. Study examines brain water diffusivity in ultra-marathon runners using MRI and blood biomarkers.

false_positive.file: Callan et al. 2012 - Dynamic visuomotor transformation involved with remote flying of a plane utilizes the 'Mirror Neuron' system.pdf
false_positive.title: Dynamic Visuomotor Transformation Involved Remote Flying of a Plane Utilizes the 'Mirror Neuron' System
false_positive.evidence: No mention of race or ethnicity in title, abstract, introduction, or methods. Paper focuses on visuomotor transformation and mirror neuron system.

false_positive.file: Millet and Millet 2012 - Ultramarathon is an outstanding model for the study of adaptive responses to extreme load and stress.pdf
false_positive.title: Ultramarathon is an outstanding model for the study of adaptive responses to extreme load and stress
false_positive.evidence: No race or ethnicity mentioned. Commentary on ultramarathon study examining adaptive responses to extreme exercise and brain changes.

false_positive.file: Möller et al. 1996 - MR flow quantification using RACE - clinical application to the carotid arteries.pdf
false_positive.title: MR Flow Quantification Using RACE: Clinical Application to the Carotid
false_positive.evidence: RACE is an acronym for 'real time acquisition and evaluation of motion,' a technical MRI sequence for measuring blood flow in carotid arteries.

false_positive.file: Rafiemanesh et al. 2023 - The Rapid Arterial oCclusion Evaluation (RACE) ... f acute ischemic stroke in emergency department - A multicenter study.pdf
false_positive.title: The Rapid Arterial oCclusion Evaluation (RACE) scale accuracy for diagnosis of acute ischemic stroke in emergency department – A multicenter study
false_positive.evidence: Paper evaluates RACE stroke screening scale accuracy in emergency department patients using MRI diagnosis. Race/ethnicity not mentioned or analyzed.

false_positive.file: Piovanelli et al. 2020 - Towards a Simplified Estimation of Muscle Activation Pattern from MRI and EMG Using Electrical Network and Graph Theory.pdf
false_positive.title: Towards a Simplified Estimation of Muscle Activation Pattern from MRI and EMG Using Electrical Network and Graph Theory
false_positive.evidence: Email address su@race.t.u-tokyo.ac.jp contains 'race' as acronym for 'Research Into Artifacts Center for Engineering'; no race/ethnicity discussion in paper.

false_positive.file: Claessen et al. 2014 - Right ventricular fatigue developing during endurance exercise - an exercise cardiac magnetic resonance study.pdf
false_positive.title: Right Ventricular Fatigue Developing during Endurance Exercise: An Exercise Cardiac Magnetic Resonance Study
false_positive.evidence: Race refers to 'endurance race' (cycling event), not racial/ethnic groups. No racial or ethnic classification of participants.

false_positive.file: Race et al. 2009 - Multiple forms of learning yield temporally distinct electrophysiological repetition effects.pdf
false_positive.title: Multiple Forms of Learning Yield Temporally Distinct Electrophysiological Repetition Effects
false_positive.evidence: Author surname 'Race' appears in title and throughout as first author; no race/ethnicity variable is analyzed or discussed in the paper.

cannot_determine.title: Halperin et al. 1993 - An analysis of radiotherapy data from the CNS can ... atment of patients with primary malignant brain tumors. The CNS canc[...]

cannot_determine.file: Enhancing Emotion Regulation and Brain ...mized Controlled Trial - ScienceDirect.pdf
cannot_determine.title: Enhancing Emotion Regulation and Brain Connectivity in Adolescent Girls Through Parental Emotion Socialization: A Randomized Controlled Trial
cannot_determine.evidence: No substantive mention of race or ethnicity in the provided text; title and abstract focus on emotion regulation intervention in adolescent girls.

cannot_determine.file: Santini et al. 2021 - Analysis of hippocampal subfields in sickle cell disease using ultrahigh field MRI.pdf
cannot_determine.title: Analysis of hippocampal subfields in sickle cell disease using ultrahigh field MRI
cannot_determine.evidence: Healthy controls were age, race, and sex matched to the participants with SCD.
